summary.intent: Implement task #280: a framework-agnostic Calendar core component (state machine, grid math, connect API, tests) for the ars-ui Rust component library, then port all implementation learnings back to the spec.
summary.outcome: Calendar component fully implemented (mod.rs, grid.rs, tests.rs) with 130 passing tests (unit, snapshot, proptest, spec-conformance), spec updated to match the real API surface, and PR #688 created. Post-Codex-review, 5 bugs were fixed (HTML disabled vs aria-disabled, missing cell_trigger_attrs_for, paging not advancing focused_date, disabled guard blocking FocusOut) and the fixes committed.

repo: CalendarDate has no Ord impl, so BTreeSet<CalendarDate> is unusable; need a SelectedDates newtype using CalendarDate::compare() to maintain sorted order.
Callback::new requires Args: 'static and cannot wrap &T reference arguments; a new Callback::new_ref constructor accepting HRTB closures (for<'a> Fn(&'a T)) was needed in ars-core.
The idiomatic pattern for discarding send results is drop(svc.send(...)), not let _ = svc.send(...) — clippy enforces this distinction.
ComponentIds replaces manual heading_id/grid_id string fields across component connect APIs.
spec validation runs via cargo xtask spec validate (checks 340 files); pre-push gate is cargo xci-fast.
The real ars-i18n CalendarDate API uses u8 + Result<_, CalendarError> returns (not NonZero<u8> + infallible); add_days/add_months propagate errors.
Snapshot tests use cargo insta; after cargo insta review --accept, verify no unrelated snapshots were accepted accidentally.
cargo-mutants baseline fails when pre-existing unrelated tests (meter/progress snapshots) break; workaround is to skip the baseline check.
GitHub project board status updates go through the GraphQL API (gh api graphql), not standard gh issue commands.
ConnectApi pattern: Machine::connect is not called directly in tests; use service().connect via svc variable.

code.finding: Callback::new signature requires Args: 'static, blocking use with &CalendarDate. Added new_ref constructor for Callback<dyn for<'a> Fn(&'a T) -> Out + Send + Sync> to support higher-ranked closure predicates.
Unavailable cells must only set aria-disabled, not HTML disabled — HTML disabled removes the focus model entirely, but unavailable dates must remain focusable per spec.
month_step_plan must advance focused_date by the same step as visible_month/year; failing to do so leaves no cell with tabindex=0 after paging, breaking keyboard re-entry.
Top-level ctx.disabled guard must allow focus-lifecycle events (FocusOut) through; blocking them leaves stale Focused state if the calendar is disabled mid-focus.
cell_trigger_attrs used the first-visible-month for outside-month detection. Refactored to cell_trigger_attrs_for(date, offset) with a shared inner accepting an offset parameter.

workflow: TDD order: write named failing tests first (compile errors expected), implement to make them pass, accept snapshots, then verify clippy and coverage gates.
Spawn parallel exploration agents for large spec reads to protect main context window; then verify disagreements with targeted grep.
AskUserQuestion during plan phase to settle scope ambiguities (multi-select inclusion, hover-events scope) before writing any code.
Worktree-based development isolates feature work; all edits target the worktree path, not the main workspace.
Autonomous loop (ScheduleWakeup) keeps work moving between user interactions; irreversible actions (push, PR) wait for explicit approval.
Codex review is triggered via waiting-for-codex-review skill after PR creation; poll output file for verdict, then address findings as a structured task list.
Coverage check before and after bug fixes confirms no regression; mutation tests catch logic gaps that line coverage misses.

friction: CalendarDate missing Ord impl was a silent gap in the spec — the spec specified BTreeSet<CalendarDate> which is uncompilable, only caught during implementation.
Callback::new's 'static bound on Args is a fundamental API limitation that required extending ars-core; this was not discoverable from the type alias alone.
cargo-mutants repeatedly hit pre-existing baseline failures (meter/progress snapshots unrelated to Calendar), requiring multiple workaround attempts before finding --skip-baseline.
Batch sed replacement of let _ = svc.send(...) → drop(svc.send(...)) left .bak files and required manual cleanup.
cargo insta review --accept accidentally accepted unrelated data_display snapshot regressions that had to be separately reverted.
Spec used NonZero<u8> and nzu8() helper throughout but the actual ars-i18n API uses plain u8 + Result — required touching nearly every code example in the spec.
open_items: Shift+Space range-from-anchor and Ctrl+A select-all multi-select keyboard extras are unimplemented — they require an 'anchor' concept not yet defined in the spec; flagged as deferred pending spec refinement.

Hover events (Event::HoverDate/HoverEnd), is_range/range_start/range_end context fields, and on_cell_hover/on_grid_mouseleave API methods were removed as range-calendar drift — need proper spec design for the future RangeCalendar component.
Mutation test full run was never completed due to tooling issues (baseline failures, long runtime); partial runs showed 0 MISSED across 159 outcomes but a clean full run is still outstanding.
Codex review thread resolution and re-triggering Codex after the bug-fix commit was the last action in the transcript — PR thread cleanup may still be pending.
